title: Atylosia albicans
intro: Atylosia albicans is a plant species native to tropical Africa, classified as a legume belonging to the family Fabaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate conditions such as fever, pain, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Atylosia albicans include flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its pharmacological activities. In traditional medicine, Atylosia albicans is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions, with the most common preparations being a leaf infusion for fever and a root decoction for pain relief.
